initiator_prompt: [MESSAGE] Send a message to {assistant_role} to begin discussing the current task.
[CRITICAL] Write ONLY a message to instruct {assistant_role}. Do NOT simulate any response.
Your message will be sent to {assistant_role}. Wait for their actual response. DO NOT write both sides of the conversation.
[RESULT TAGS] Both parties must NOT use <result> tags until reaching agreement.
When BOTH agree on a conclusion, {assistant_role} should output: <result>conclusion</result>
[EFFICIENCY] Be concise. Avoid excessive back-and-forth. When agreement is reached, conclude immediately.
[INSTRUCT {assistant_role}] The message must meet the following requirements:
- Explain the task: introduce the [PHASE TASK] and basic context of the project
- Includes how the phase works, [RESULT TAGS] usage rules and [EFFICIENCY] requirements
- This instruction should not have any result XML tags with content, as this will cause the phase to end prematurely.
Example WRONG: 'Output <result>modality</result> to end this phase when we reach a consensus.'
Example CORRECT: 'When we reach a consensus, put the conclusion into <result></result> to end this stage; this tag must not contain any content beforehand.'
[PHASE TASK] Analyze user requirements and recommend the best product modality.
[CONTEXT] {context}
DemandAnalysis.context:
User Request: {task}

Available Modalities:
- Image: charts, graphs, visualizations
- Document: .docx files
- PowerPoint: .pptx files
- Excel: .xlsx files
- PDF: .pdf files
- Website: .html files
- Application: Python software, tools, games
- Dashboard: real-time information panels
- Mind Map: idea organization diagrams

[PHASE TASK] Recommend the most suitable programming language for this project.
[CONTEXT] {context}
[TASK] Design project architecture and write framework code (without implementation).
[USER REQUIREMENTS]
- Task: {task}
- Modality: {modality}
- Language: {language}
- Description: {description}
[IMPORTANT]
- Your workspace is already configured with git and tea CLI
- You can refer to Gitea skill for tea usage details
[WORKFLOW]
1. Design project architecture:
- Directory structure
- Main modules and their responsibilities
- Data models (if applicable)
- API endpoints (if applicable)
2. Write framework code WITHOUT implementation:
- Create main entry point files with TODO/placeholder
- Set up project structure with empty modules
- Add basic configuration files
3. Create repository and push:
- Use 'tea repo create' to create PUBLIC repository
- Commit and push framework code to main branch (NO PR)
4. After completing, output <result>Done</result>
[TASK] Add Programmer as collaborator and notify them.
[IMPORTANT]
- The repository was already created in CodingDesign phase
- Your workspace is already configured with git and tea CLI
[GITEA WORKFLOW]
1. Use 'tea repo add-collaborator' to add Programmer as a collaborator to the repository
[MESSAGE] After adding collaborator, send a message to {assistant_role}.
[CRITICAL] Write ONLY a message to instruct {assistant_role}. Do NOT simulate any response.
Your message will be sent to {assistant_role}. Wait for their response. DO NOT write both sides of the conversation.
[INSTRUCT {assistant_role}] The message must:
- Include the repository URL
- Ask {assistant_role} to confirm whether they can access the repository
- Tell {assistant_role} to review the framework code and start implementing
- Tell {assistant_role} they must NOT output <result> tags until they confirm access
- Do NOT include <result> tags with content in this message, as this will skip the phase
[TERMINATION] After {assistant_role} confirms they can access the repository, output <result>Done</result>.
[MESSAGE] Send a message to {assistant_role} to begin discussing the current task.
[CRITICAL] Write ONLY a message to instruct {assistant_role}. Do NOT simulate any response.
Your message will be sent to {assistant_role}. Wait for their actual response. DO NOT write both sides of the conversation.
[RESULT TAGS]
- Do NOT output <result> tags until PR is created and approved
- When the current code already meets user requirements, do not make unnecessary modifications. Output <result>Done</result> directly.
[EFFICIENCY] Be concise. Complete the task efficiently.
[INSTRUCT {assistant_role}] The message must meet the following requirements:
- Identify code deficiencies or missing features based on user requirements, and clearly specify what needs to be improved
- Explain the phase mechanism: Programmer will improve code and create PR, CTO reviews and approves. Repeat until all issues are resolved
- Tell {assistant_role} they must NOT output <result> tags. Whether this phase ends is determined solely by you (CTO)
- Do NOT include <result> tags with content in this message. This will cause the phase to be skipped
Example WRONG: "You MUST NOT output <result>Done</result>"
Example CORRECT: "DO NOT output any <result> tags"
[PHASE TASK] Based on the user's requirements and current code, instruct Programmer to improve the code and create PRs until you approve.
[GITEA WORKFLOW]
- Programmer creates a new branch for this task as needed
- Programmer writes or modifies code
- Programmer uses 'git add', 'git commit', 'git push' to save changes
- Programmer uses 'tea pr create' to create Pull Request
- You review and decide whether to accept the PR
[CONTEXT] {context}
[TASK] Add Code Reviewer as a collaborator to the repository with write permission.
[IMPORTANT]
- Use tea CLI to add Code Reviewer as a collaborator
- Your workspace is already configured with git and tea CLI
Example: tea api -X PUT -f permission=write /repos/OWNER/REPO/collaborators/USERNAME
- Replace OWNER/REPO with actual repository owner and name from 'tea repo list'
[GITEA WORKFLOW]
1. Use 'tea repo list' to find the repository
2. Add Code Reviewer as a collaborator with write permission
[MESSAGE] After adding collaborator, send a message to {assistant_role}.
[CRITICAL] Write ONLY a message to instruct {assistant_role}. Do NOT simulate any response.
Your message will be sent to {assistant_role}. Wait for their response. DO NOT write both sides of the conversation.
[INSTRUCT {assistant_role}] The message must meet the following requirements:
- Include the repository URL
- Describe the user requirements: {task}
- Ask {assistant_role} to verify access and confirm readiness
- Do NOT include <result> tags with content in this message, as this will skip the phase
Example WRONG: "You MUST NOT output <result>Done</result>"
Example CORRECT: "DO NOT output any <result> tags"
[RESULT TAGS] Termination conditions:
- Do NOT output <result> tags until {assistant_role} confirms access
- When {assistant_role} confirms they have access, output <result>Done</result>
After {assistant_role} confirms, output <result>Done</result> to conclude.
[TASK] Conduct code review and ensure code quality.
[USER REQUIREMENTS] {task}
[MESSAGE] Send a message to {assistant_role} to begin the code review.
[CRITICAL] Write ONLY a message to instruct {assistant_role}. Do NOT simulate any response.
Your message will be sent to {assistant_role}. Wait for their actual response. DO NOT write both sides of the conversation.
[INSTRUCT {assistant_role}] Each round, you must:
1. First check if there is an open PR: use 'tea pulls list' to find PRs
2. If NO PR exists: Analyze the current code in the repository for problems:
- Read the code files to understand the implementation
- Identify issues, bugs, or areas for improvement
- Tell Programmer what problems exist and need fixing
3. If PR exists: Review the code changes in the PR:
- Check what problems the PR would introduce if merged
- Identify any issues with the proposed changes
4. If code has issues: ask Programmer to fix them
5. If code is qualified and meets user requirements: output <result>Done</result>
- Do NOT output <result> tags until review is complete
Example WRONG: "You MUST NOT output <result>Done</result>"
Example CORRECT: "DO NOT output any <result> tags"
[RESULT TAGS] Termination conditions:
- Do NOT output <result> tags until code review is complete and all issues are resolved
- When code meets requirements and no issues remain, output <result>Done</result>
[CHECKLIST]
1. All imports present and correct
2. All methods fully implemented
3. Necessary comments present
4. No potential bugs
5. Meets user requirements
6. Logic is sound
After review is complete, output <result>Done</result> to conclude.
